141.definition: A completely empty space — nothing at all
Existing at the very beginning of time
A kingdom or world with its own rules and creatures.

142.definition: Giving up something valuable — sometimes very valuable — for a greater purpose.
A prediction or vision of what will happen in the future.
Ancient carved symbols used for writing and magic in Norse culture.

143.definition: Reading signs — especially the behavior of birds — to predict the future or receive guidance from the gods.
A final demand that must be met
Handling a difficult situation carefully to avoid making things worse.

144.definition: To talk through a disagreement and try to reach an agreement both sides can live with.

146.definition: Something you have that gives you power in a negotiation or difficult situation.
Support or protection given by someone powerful.
Complete power and independence — ruling yourself

148.definition: Glowing with warmth and light — giving off a brightness that others can feel.
Being sent away from home as a punishment

156.definition: A person who is highly skilled at making things by hand.
Deeply desired by many people — wanted badly enough that people might do questionable things to get it.
To lose something or give it up as a penalty.
